machine.name: Home Transportation Barrier Monitor
machine.description: Monitors transportation access barriers that prevent low- and medium-income home health patients from attending medical appointments, filling prescriptions, and accessing community services. Transportation is a top driver of missed care in rural and low-income urban settings — more predictive of care gaps than insurance status alone. Output at [2015:2019] feeds the Home Social Isolation Monitor alongside the Medication Cost Barrier Monitor.

metadata.domain: Home Health — Social Determinants / Transportation Access
metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Rural and low-income urban patients; elderly without vehicles; patients relying on public transit or ride programs in transit deserts

sensorNormalization.appointment_attendance_norm: 0.0=no appointments attended in past period (complete care avoidance due to transport), 0.5=~50% attendance (some missed due to transport), 1.0=all scheduled appointments attended
sensorNormalization.transit_access_norm: 0.0=no viable transit options (rural transit desert; no car; no rideshare), 0.5=limited access (one-way possible, return uncertain; shared ride program with waitlist), 1.0=reliable transit or personal vehicle available
sensorNormalization.missed_critical_care_norm: 0.0=critical care missed (dialysis, chemotherapy, specialty follow-up) due to transport, 0.5=non-critical missed (primary care, routine labs), 1.0=no care missed due to transport
sensorNormalization.proximity_norm: 0.0=>30 miles from nearest relevant provider with no transit, 0.5=10–30 miles with unreliable transit, 1.0=within 5 miles or transport resolved

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning HomeTransportationBarrierMonitor matures.

rules.description: CRITICAL_ACCESS_FAILURE: patient missing critical care due to transportation barrier at [2015:2019]
TRANSIT_DEPENDENT_RISK: care access contingent on fragile transit arrangement at [2015:2019]
TRANSPORTATION_ADEQUATE: care access not transport-limited at [2015:2019]

sequences.name: Access Collapse: MOBILE+ → STRAINED → IMPAIRED → CRITICAL_ACCESS_FAILURE

metadata.description: Tracks progressive transportation access collapse. In rural and low-income urban areas, loss of a vehicle (breakdown, repossession) or transit program changes can instantly eliminate care access for patients dependent on dialysis, oncology, or specialty chronic disease management. The MOBILE+ baseline requires confirmed transportation before collapse can be signaled.

metadata.name: MOBILE
metadata.description: MOBILE+ — reliable transportation; appointments attended; no critical care missed; reasonable provider proximity or transit access. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: STRAINED
metadata.description: STRAINED — appointment attendance declining; transit access unreliable; ride program waitlist; non-critical care being missed.

metadata.name: IMPAIRED
metadata.description: IMPAIRED — critical care being missed (dialysis, specialty); no transit options available; proximity barrier severe. Fires CRITICAL_ACCESS_FAILURE.
CRITICAL_ACCESS_FAILURE: patient missing critical care due to transportation barrier at [2015:2019]
metadata.action: NEMT (non-emergency medical transport) referral; volunteer driver program; care coordination for telemedicine substitution where appropriate

metadata.description: Fires TRANSIT_DEPENDENT_RISK for patients whose care access depends entirely on unreliable public transit or shared ride programs — a fragile state that one schedule change or route cut can break. Targets rural and low-income urban transit deserts.

metadata.name: TRANSIT_DEPENDENT
metadata.description: TRANSIT_DEPENDENT+ — patient currently attending care but solely reliant on public transit or paratransit with no backup option. isInitial A*A = A+: fires TRANSIT_DEPENDENT_RISK each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Rural patient on county medical transport van; one missed pickup = missed dialysis

metadata.description: TRANSIT_DEPENDENT_RISK: care access contingent on fragile transit arrangement at [2015:2019]
metadata.action: Backup transportation plan; volunteer driver registration; identify family/neighbor support; telemedicine option assessment

metadata.description: Fires TRANSPORTATION_ADEQUATE when patient has reliable transportation and is attending scheduled care consistently. May reflect personal vehicle, Medicaid NEMT, or stable transit with high attendance history.

metadata.name: ADEQUATE
metadata.description: ADEQUATE+ — transportation reliable; no missed care due to transport; reasonable proximity or NEMT coverage. Fires TRANSPORTATION_ADEQUATE each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Medicaid NEMT enrolled; or family member providing consistent rides; or rural transit schedule matched to care schedule

metadata.description: TRANSPORTATION_ADEQUATE: care access not transport-limited at [2015:2019]
metadata.action: Social isolation monitor registers transportation stable

inputSequences.name: Rural Vehicle Breakdown — Dialysis Risk
inputSequences.description: Patient's only vehicle breaks down; rural area has no public transit; two dialysis sessions missed in one week; NEMT referral resolves access.
